message:
fix(connectors): Enforce introspection-backed capability lookup and safe integer conversions

Updates all 28 connectors with two cross-cutting safety improvements:

1. Capability ID resolution now validates against introspection results
   before parsing, ensuring only known operations from the connector's
   introspection surface can be invoked. Previously, operation strings
   were parsed directly to CapabilityId without verifying the operation
   existed in the connector's declared capabilities.

2. Integer conversions from JSON u64 values to u32 parameters now use
   u32::try_from(v).ok() instead of unchecked `v as u32` casts, preventing
   silent overflow bugs when input payloads contain values exceeding u32::MAX.
   Affected connectors: browser, discord, figma, github, gmail, google-ai,
   google-calendar, kubernetes, microsoft365.

Both changes improve security (no unvalidated capability execution) and
robustness (no silent integer truncation) across the connector fleet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -853,7 +853,7 @@ impl GitHubConnector {
             milestone: input
                 .get("milestone")
                 .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
-                .map(|v| v as u32),
+                .and_then(|v| u32::try_from(v).ok()),
         };
 
         let issue = client
@@ -1232,7 +1232,7 @@ fn require_u32(input: &serde_json::Value, field: &str) -> FcpResult<u32> {
     input
         .get(field)
         .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
-        .map(|v| v as u32)
+        .and_then(|v| u32::try_from(v).ok())
         .ok_or(FcpError::InvalidRequest {
             code: 1003,
             message: format!("Missing required field: {field}"),
